entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Dual purpose crop yielding food (grain) and fodder (stover) grown in marginal environments of Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa

entities.name: FODDER QUALITY
entities.description: Characteristics of the stover used for animal feed, which can be improved through breeding

entities.name: STOVER DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The degree to which sorghum stover can be broken down during digestion, affecting livestock output

entities.name: LIVESTOCK OUTPUT
entities.description: The productivity of livestock, which increases with improved stover digestibility

entities.name: IN VITRO ORGANIC MATTER DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: A key stover quality trait measured as IVOMD%

entities.name: TEST WEIGHT
entities.description: A grain quality trait measured in grams (TW-g)

entities.name: STALK YIELD
entities.description: The amount of stover produced per hectare (SY-Kg/ha)

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per hectare (GY-Kg/ha)

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A physical growth trait of the sorghum plant (PH)

entities.name: WATER REGIMES
entities.description: Different levels of water availability, specifically control and stress, used in evaluation

entities.name: CONTROL
entities.description: A water regime representing normal water availability

entities.name: STRESS
entities.description: A water regime representing limited water availability

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Geographic region where sorghum is grown

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Geographic region where sorghum is grown

entities.name: ICRISAT-PATANCHERU
entities.description: The location where mapping population parents were evaluated

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The application of genome-wide study to expedite genetic improvement of crops

entities.name: TRAIT DISCOVERY TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used in breeding programs to identify and improve specific traits

entities.name: GENETIC MAPPING
entities.description: The process of identifying genomic regions related to specific traits

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic effort to improve the genetic makeup of a crop

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATION PARENTS
entities.description: A set of 24 parents evaluated to characterize fodder quality traits

entities.name: ICSV1
entities.description: A parental sorghum line with contrasting IVOMD values used for genetic mapping

entities.name: ICSV700
entities.description: A parental sorghum line with contrasting IVOMD values used for genetic mapping

relationships.description: Sorghum is evaluated for fodder quality traits
Sorghum yields grain
Sorghum yields stover/stalk
Stover digestibility is a component of fodder quality
Stover digestibility increases livestock output
IVOMD is a key stover quality trait
Sorghum was evaluated under different water regimes
Control is a type of water regime
Stress is a type of water regime
Genomics can expedite genetic improvement of fodder quality
Trait discovery tools can expedite genetic improvement of fodder quality
Genetic mapping is used to identify genomic regions related to fodder quality traits
Positive correlation observed between stalk yield and IVOMD
Positive correlation observed between plant height and stalk yield
ICSV1 has contrasting IVOMD values for genetic mapping
ICSV700 has contrasting IVOMD values for genetic mapping
Sorghum is grown in Asia
Sorghum is grown in Sub-Saharan Africa
Sorghum parents were evaluated at ICRISAT-Patancheru
Parents are specific genotypes of sorghum
Genetic mapping is performed using mapping population parents
